description: baseline: ResNet152 full fine-tune, dropout=0.5, lr=1e-3/backbone_lr=1e-5, bs=96, cosine LR, label_smooth=0.15
MixUp alpha=0.2: ties baseline but adds complexity
TrivialAugmentWide replaces manual augmentation chain (+0.010)
img_size=256 batch_size=64: larger input resolution (+0.007 vs prev best)
CutMix p=0.5 alpha=1.0: strong regularization (+0.023 vs prev best)
backbone_lr=1e-4: peaked early (epoch 26) then degraded
dropout=0.3 (was 0.5): less over-regularization with CutMix (+0.007)
T_max=45: LR decays too fast, hurts mid-run learning
img_size=288 bs=48: only ~35 epochs in budget, too few to converge
no RandomErasing: hurt performance, RandomErasing complements CutMix
SWA epochs 35-47: averaged post-peak decline phase, SWA≤single checkpoint
CutMix p=0.7: over-regularizes, train_acc ~0.80 vs 0.86 at p=0.5
label_smoothing=0.05: no improvement, 0.15 is better with CutMix
2-layer head 2048->512->100: worse than single linear, simple head is better
ResNet50 (vs ResNet152): more epochs (96) but lower capacity, peaked at epoch 14 then plateaued
warmup(5ep)+cosine: fewer effective epochs, warmup overhead hurts
backbone_weight_decay=1e-4 (was 1e-3): more forgetting of pretrained features, worse
crop scale=(0.08,1.0): too aggressive, small patches confuse model (+0 vs best)
MixUp(25%)+CutMix(25%)+plain(50%): diluted CutMix effect, worse than CutMix alone
EMA (per-epoch update, decay=0.9997): bug - EMA was 98.6% initial weights after 47 epochs
EMA per-batch (decay=0.9997): bug - BN running stats not updated via state_dict
EMA per-batch state_dict (decay=0.9997): averaging smooths out peak, worse than single best ckpt
dropout=0.1: over-fits without sufficient head regularization
img_size=448 bs=16: larger input resolution (+0.013 vs prev best); evaluate.py called with --img_size 448
bs=32 lr=5e-4 backbone_lr=5e-5: peak at epoch 21 (vs ep6 before) more stable convergence (+0.007)
bs=48: peaked ep11 then plateaued, same epoch count as bs=32 (data-load bottleneck), worse than bs=32
epochs=35 (T_max=35): LR fully exhausted by ep29, low-LR fine-tuning gave no gain, same 0.930 ceiling
backbone_lr=1e-4: too aggressive, noisy training, peaked ep22 at 0.9267 < 0.930
RandAugment(2,9) vs TrivialAugmentWide: more ops but worse, TrivialAugmentWide has better diversity
GeM pooling (p=3, learnable) replaces AvgPool: focuses on discriminative features (+0.003)
